title: UT Vols Football
description: One of the largest stadiums in the world (101,915 capacity) on the banks of the Tennessee River. Home games on Saturdays in the fall. The Vol Navy tradition has fans arriving by boat.

title: UT Vols Men's Basketball
venueName: Thompson-Boling Arena at Food City Center
description: SEC basketball in one of the largest on-campus arenas in the country (21,000+ capacity). Home games on weekday evenings and Saturdays, November through March.

title: Lady Vols Basketball
venueName: Thompson-Boling Arena at Food City Center
description: Tennessee's legendary Lady Vols women's basketball program — one of the most decorated in college hoops history. Home games November through March.

title: Knoxville Smokies Baseball
description: Double-A affiliate of the Chicago Cubs, now playing at the brand-new Covenant Health Park in the Magnolia Warehouse District, opened April 2025. 6,355-seat ballpark steps from the Old City.

title: Knoxville Ice Bears Hockey
description: Southern Professional Hockey League action in the 6,500-seat Civic Coliseum. Affordable, loud, and one of the best live sports values in East Tennessee. Home games Friday and Saturday nights, October through March.

title: Blue Plate Special (Free Live Music)
description: Free live music broadcast every weekday at noon from the Knoxville Visitor Center. A beloved institution featuring bluegrass, folk, country, and Americana. On Fridays the show moves to Barley's Taproom in the Old City.

title: Live Theater & Broadway Shows
description: A grand 1928 atmospheric movie palace restored as Knoxville's premier performing arts venue. Hosts Broadway touring shows, concerts, dance, film screenings, and the Big Ears Festival. ~1,600 capacity.

title: Live Concerts & Touring Acts
description: Historic 700-capacity intimate venue on Gay Street hosting national touring artists across rock, Americana, folk, and comedy. One of Knoxville's most beloved live music rooms.

title: Live Music Concerts
description: Converted industrial concert venue near the Old City with 1,200-capacity indoor space plus an 8,000 sq ft outdoor courtyard. State-of-the-art sound, private suites, and a strong touring schedule.

title: Live Music & Craft Beer
description: Old City cornerstone with craft beer on tap and live music nearly every night spanning Americana, blues, bluegrass, and rock. Also hosts the WDVX Blue Plate Special on Fridays.

title: Market Square Farmers Market
description: Producer-only open-air market on historic Market Square with ~140 local vendors. Main season (May–Oct): Saturdays 9am–1pm and Wednesdays 10am–1pm. Winter market (Nov–Apr): Saturdays 10am–2pm.

title: Food Hall Dining & Drinks
description: Knoxville's first large-scale food hall in the historic Regas Square building. 15,000+ sq ft of rotating and permanent restaurant stalls, a full bar, and communal seating in the Depot neighborhood.
price: Free to enter · pay per dish

title: Urban Nature Preserve & Trails
description: 275-acre urban wildlife sanctuary with 10 miles of trails, seasonal kayaking at Mead's Quarry Lake, rock climbing, and mountain biking. A green oasis 10 minutes from downtown — $5 parking.

title: Tennessee Riverwalk
description: Paved riverside greenway running 3+ miles along the Tennessee River through downtown past Neyland Stadium and Volunteer Landing. Popular for walking, running, and cycling. Connects to 100+ miles of greenways.

title: Sunsphere & World's Fair Park
description: Iconic gold globe from the 1982 World's Fair with a free observation deck and sweeping 360° city views. Surrounded by a beautiful park used for concerts, Knox Pride, and the Knoxville Marathon.

title: First Friday Art Walk
description: Monthly celebration of local art on the first Friday of every month. Galleries, studios, and shops stay open late, street performers fill the streets, and many venues offer free wine and food.

title: Free Art Museum & Exhibitions
description: East Tennessee's primary fine art museum, free to the public, adjacent to World's Fair Park. Focuses on contemporary and historical Appalachian and Southeast American art with rotating special exhibitions.

title: Regional History Museum
description: Three floors of permanent and rotating exhibits on Cherokee history, Civil War, early settlement, and regional heritage. Admission free on Sundays. Located on Gay Street downtown.

title: City Zoo & Wildlife
description: Full-service accredited zoo in East Knoxville with large mammals, rare red pandas, reptiles, and birds. Seasonal events include Boo at the Zoo (October) and Holiday Lights. Open daily 9am–5pm.

title: Big Ears Festival
description: Nationally acclaimed experimental and avant-garde music festival across 20 downtown venues — Tennessee Theatre, Bijou, KMA, and more. ~250 performances over 4 days each March.

title: Dogwood Arts Festival
description: Three-day outdoor juried art festival in late April at World's Fair Park with 100+ artists, live music, a maker market, and hands-on workshops. Part of the broader month-long Dogwood Arts season.

title: Tennessee Valley Fair
description: A 100+ year old traditional fair every September at Chilhowee Park. Carnival rides, livestock competitions, midway, food vendors, live music on multiple stages, and grandstand shows. One of the largest fairs in the Southeast.

title: Knox Pride Fest
description: Knoxville's annual LGBTQ+ pride celebration at World's Fair Park each June. Live entertainment, vendors, community programming, and a Friday night parade through downtown.

title: Covenant Health Knoxville Marathon
description: Boston Marathon qualifier in mid-April, starting at the Sunsphere. Offers marathon, half marathon, 4-person relay, 5K, and Kids Run. Course winds through downtown and surrounding neighborhoods.

title: Market Square Summer Concert Series
description: Free outdoor concerts on Market Square on Friday evenings throughout the summer. Local and regional acts, food trucks, and a festive family atmosphere in the heart of downtown.

title: Country Dancing & Line Dancing
description: The biggest honky tonk in East Tennessee. Line dancing lessons, live country music, a mechanical bull, and dancing until last call. A Knoxville institution for 30+ years.

title: Indie & Underground Shows
description: Beloved DIY venue in the Old City hosting indie, punk, experimental, and underground acts. All-ages, no-frills, and consistently one of Knoxville's best rooms for catching emerging talent.

title: Live Music Rooftop Bar
description: Multi-story bar on Market Square with a rooftop patio and live music almost every night — jazz, blues, folk, and rock. One of the most distinctive spots in downtown Knoxville.

title: Clingmans Dome Sunrise Hike
location: Clingmans Dome Rd, Great Smoky Mountains NP
description: Hike to the highest point in the Smokies (6,643 ft) for a breathtaking sunrise above the clouds. The paved summit trail is 0.5 miles each way. Plan for a 2am departure from Knoxville.

title: Silent Disco
description: Put on wireless headphones and choose your channel — three DJs, one dance floor, zero speakers. Wild, hilarious, and totally addictive.

title: Honky Tonk Thursday
description: $5 cover, cheap beer, and two-step lessons starting at 9pm every Thursday. The real country scene in North Knox — no tourists, just locals.

title: Wednesday Jazz Night
description: Intimate jazz sessions in the lounge at the Mill & Mine on select Wednesday nights. Rotating local jazz trio, craft cocktails, and a low-key crowd.

title: Underground Electronic Events
description: Rotating underground electronic music events at warehouses and bars around the Old City. Watch local Instagram pages for location reveals 24 hours before.

title: Rooftop Yoga
description: Sunday morning yoga on the rooftop terrace of the historic Oliver Hotel with panoramic city views. $15, BYOM. Sign up via The Oliver website.

title: Sunday Bluegrass Jam
description: Informal bluegrass jam session in the park every Sunday afternoon. Bring an instrument or just listen. All skill levels welcome, BYOB.

title: Roller Derby Bouts
description: East Tennessee's flat-track roller derby league hosts bouts monthly. Loud, chaotic, and incredibly fun. One of Knoxville's most unique and affordable live events.

title: Full Moon Drum Circle
description: Community drum circle and bonfire gathering on the night of every full moon in Sequoyah Hills Park. Free, open, and welcoming to all.

title: Midnight Ramble
description: Late-night acoustic sessions that pop up around Market Square at midnight on warm summer weekends.

title: Secret Cinema Club
description: Monthly underground film screening series showing cult classics, foreign films, and experimental cinema. Location revealed 48 hours before via newsletter.

title: Late-Night Private Karaoke
description: Knoxville's first all-hours karaoke bar in a cozy basement on Gay Street. Open-stage format (not private rooms) Tuesday through Sunday nights starting at 8 PM. Full bar and grilled cheese menu.
price: Cover charge · check website for rates

title: Springboard Open Mic Comedy
description: Free weekly open mic comedy night hosted by True Grit Comedy. Sign-ups at 7:30 PM, show at 8 PM through midnight. 7-minute sets, any style. Beginners welcome — no experience needed.

title: Jazz on the Square
description: Free outdoor jazz concerts on Market Square every Tuesday evening in May, June, and September (7–9 PM). Presented by Axle Logistics. Bring a lawn chair — all ages welcome.

title: Illusions The Drag Brunch
description: Monthly drag show brunch at Market Square featuring celebrity impersonators and tributes to Madonna, Cher, Tina Turner, and more. Brunch cocktails, mimosas, and a full performance included.

title: Open Mic Night
description: Weekly acoustic singer-songwriter open mic at this beloved Old City whisky bar every Wednesday. Sign-ups at 6 PM, show at 7 PM. Up to 20 performers, piano available. Free to attend and perform.

title: Tuesday Trivia
description: Free pub trivia every Tuesday 8–10 PM at this self-pour taproom with 68 rotating digital taps. Hosted by Timeless Entertainment — top 3 teams win gift cards, and players get 15% off their tab.

title: Open Mic Monday
description: Monday open mic hosted by Chris Marshall at Gypsy Circus's cider and mead taproom. Free to play and attend. Also: Singo music bingo Tuesdays and free trivia Thursdays at the same spot.

title: Trivia Thursday
description: Free Thursday trivia at this Old North cider and mead taproom, hosted by The Trivia Guys. All ages welcome. Also: open mic Mondays and Singo music bingo Tuesdays — free events every weeknight.

title: Trivia Monday
description: Free weekly trivia every Monday at The Hill, a Fort Sanders institution since 2009 known for award-winning wings. Hosted by Challenge Entertainment — top teams win prizes.

title: Trivia Tuesday
description: Free Tuesday trivia followed by live music at this UT/Fort Sanders neighborhood bar. Hosted by Challenge Entertainment. Also: Singo music bingo on Thursdays with a DJ set after.

title: Trivia Wednesday
description: Free weekly trivia at 7 PM at the Cumberland Ave Mellow Mushroom — historic home of Knox Trivia Guys, founded here in 2002. Hosted by Challenge Entertainment. Great pizza + competitive quiz.

title: Divaz & Kingz Drag Show
description: Weekly Thursday drag show at Knoxville's iconic LGBTQ+ club in Happy Holler. Happy hour 7–9 PM; drag shows at 11:30 PM and 1 AM. Free on Thursdays. Also performs Fridays and Saturdays.

title: Trivia Wednesday
description: Free weekly trivia at 7 PM at this Happy Holler neighborhood favorite, hosted by Challenge Entertainment. Flatbread pizzas, craft beer, and a comfortable patio right on N Central.

title: Trivia Tuesday & Comedy Sunday
description: Two weekly events at this North Knoxville gastropub: Tuesday trivia at 7 PM (Challenge Entertainment, gift cards for top teams) and free Sunday comedy with Laugh Alive at 7:30 PM featuring local and touring comedians.

title: Family Movie Night
description: Free Tuesday Family Movie Night 6–9 PM at Yee-Haw's expansive North Knox beer garden with an outdoor amphitheater, fire pits, and 62 taps. Prince's Hot Chicken kitchen on site. All ages welcome.

title: Trivia, Bingo & Karaoke
description: Voted Best Bar in Knoxville — this 80s-themed cocktail bar packs in weekly events: trivia Wednesdays at 7 PM, music bingo Thursdays, and karaoke dance parties Friday and Saturday nights until midnight.

title: Trivia Monday
description: Free Monday trivia at 7 PM at this dog-friendly South Knox burger spot. Hosted by Challenge Entertainment — top teams win prizes. Craft beer, smash burgers, and competitive pub quiz vibes.

title: Singo Music Bingo
description: Free Singo (music bingo) every Monday and Thursday at 7 PM at this South Knox taco cantina, hosted by Challenge Entertainment. Monday specials: $13 domestic pitchers all day and $3 house shots.

title: Trivia Wednesday
description: Free Wednesday trivia at 7 PM hosted by Not Rocket Science Trivia at Hi-Wire's South Knox taproom. 20+ craft beers, rooftop deck, and arcade games. Gift card prizes for winning teams.

title: Private Karaoke Rooms
description: South Knoxville's first private karaoke bar with bookable rooms for up to 20 people at $30/hour. Craft cocktails, Latin-inspired bites, and a patio with yard games. Reserve in advance on weekends. Thursday–Sunday.

title: Trivia Tuesday
description: Free weekly trivia every Tuesday at 7 PM at Bearden's original brewpub, hosted by Challenge Entertainment. Southern pub fare and rotating craft taps in a neighborhood setting.

title: Nightly Karaoke
description: West Knoxville's dedicated karaoke cafe with open-stage karaoke nightly Tuesday through Saturday starting at 8 PM. Full menu, casual atmosphere — just show up and sing.

title: Drag Shows
description: West Knoxville's LGBTQ+ bar and grill hosts weekly drag shows Friday through Monday. A welcoming, inclusive neighborhood spot with a full bar and grill open until 3 AM on weekends.

title: Singo Tuesday
description: Free Singo music bingo every Tuesday at 6 PM at this Farragut sports bar with a covered amphitheater courtyard and wood-burning fireplace. Match songs to your bingo card and win prizes.

title: Trivia Wednesday
description: Free Wednesday trivia at 7 PM hosted by Not Rocket Science Trivia at this Hardin Valley craft brewery near Turkey Creek. Gift card prizes. Relaxed taproom, easy parking.

title: Trivia & Singo
description: Two free weekly events at this Northshore neighborhood bar: Tuesday trivia 7–9 PM and Sunday Singo music bingo at 7 PM. Thursday through Saturday: live acoustic and band performances.

title: Comedy Night
description: Weekly Thursday comedy showcase at this Market Square burger-and-bourbon staple. Local and regional stand-up comedians perform starting at 9 PM upstairs. No cover.

title: Open Stage Night
description: Open performance nights at Knoxville's beloved three-story Market Square pub. Rotating mix of stand-up, spoken word, and variety acts every Saturday at 10 PM. Free to attend and perform.

title: Monthly Comedy Showcase
description: Monthly comedy showcase at Old City's beloved indie venue. Touring and local stand-up comedians, small cover, and excellent craft beer. Check socials for monthly date.

title: Wednesday Night Run Club
description: Free weekly group run from the Gay Street store every Wednesday at 6 PM. Routes range 3–6 miles through downtown and the Riverwalk greenway. All paces welcome — post-run social on the patio.

title: Open Climbing
description: Knoxville's dedicated indoor climbing gym with bouldering and top-rope walls. Day passes available — no membership required. Beginner-friendly with an on-site gear shop.

title: Drop-In Yoga Classes
description: Affordable drop-in yoga in Bearden — hatha, vinyasa, yin, and restorative. Weekday morning and evening classes plus weekend options. New-student specials available.

title: Saturday Morning Run
description: One of Knoxville's longest-running free run clubs. Meets Saturday mornings at World's Fair Park — routes range 4–10 miles, all paces welcome. Visitors and tourists encouraged.

title: Knoxville Ghost Tours
description: Lantern-lit walking tour through Knoxville's haunted Old City, exploring 200+ years of dark history at locations like the historic jail, old morgue, and Victorian mansions. Tours run Fri–Sat evenings; October adds Thursday tours.

title: Brewery Tour
description: Tour Knoxville's German-inspired craft brewery in the Old City neighborhood. See the brewing process and sample fresh German-style lagers. Check their website for current tour schedule and availability.

title: Historic Tours
location: 1220 W Governor John Sevier Hwy, Knoxville
description: Free guided tours of Tennessee's last surviving 18th-century log house complex, home of Governor John Sevier. Open Saturdays April–December, 10 AM–5 PM. Costumed interpreters bring early Knoxville to life.

title: Kayak & Paddleboard Rentals
description: Rent a kayak, canoe, or stand-up paddleboard on the Tennessee River at Volunteer Landing. Scenic float past Henley Street Bridge, World's Fair Park, and the downtown skyline. No experience needed.

title: Classic Film Series
description: Monthly classic and cult film screenings inside Knoxville's historic 1928 movie palace, complete with its magnificent Wurlitzer organ. Rotating lineup of golden Hollywood, Hitchcock, and beloved cult films.

title: Movies on Market Square
description: Free outdoor movies on the big screen at Market Square on select summer Friday evenings. Pre-show festivities start at 7:30 PM; film at dusk (~8:30 PM). Bring a blanket or lawn chair — all ages welcome.

title: Art House Film Series
description: Monthly art house and documentary film screenings at the KMA, paired with curator talks and Q&A sessions. Films tie into current exhibitions or regional themes. Free with museum admission (admission is free).

title: Old City Farmers Market
description: Saturday morning neighborhood market in Knoxville's historic Old City with local produce, baked goods, artisan crafts, and small-batch food vendors. Runs spring through fall — check socials for exact location and hours.

title: Bearden Village Farmers Market
location: Kingston Pike at Bearden Village, Knoxville
description: Tuesday evening market in the Bearden Village area with local vegetables, flowers, honey, artisan foods, and handmade goods. Runs May through September.

title: Second Saturday Art Market
description: Monthly pop-up art and maker market in the Old City every second Saturday. Local artists, jewelers, potters, printmakers, and vintage dealers. Free to browse — shop local and meet the makers.

title: Open House Night
description: Knoxville's community makerspace hosts weekly open house nights — tour the facility, try tools, and meet the maker community. 3D printers, laser cutters, woodshop, electronics lab, and more. Free to visit.

title: Volunteer Cleanup
description: Monthly neighborhood beautification events organized by Keep Knoxville Beautiful. Clean up parks, plant trees, and improve community spaces across the city. All supplies provided, all welcome.

title: Monthly Mixer
description: Monthly networking mixer for Knoxville professionals in their 20s–40s. Rotating venues around downtown and midtown — follow KYP for the monthly location announcement.

title: Swing Dance Night
description: The Knoxville Swing Dance Society hosts regular swing dance nights for all skill levels — beginners welcome, no partner needed. A mix of lindy hop, east coast swing, and balboa. Check their Facebook for current venue and date.

title: Nightly Karaoke
description: Late-night karaoke most nights of the week at this South Knoxville dive bar on Sevier Ave. No frills, great crowd, a deep song library, and the kind of place where strangers become friends over a microphone.

title: House Mountain State Natural Area
description: Knox County's highest point at 2,100 ft, with two well-marked loop trails (3.5 and 6 miles) offering panoramic views of the Great Smoky Mountains and downtown Knoxville. Free, open sunrise to sunset. One of East Tennessee's best accessible summit hikes.

title: Rossini Festival
description: Knoxville's beloved annual celebration of Italian culture on Market Square each April. Free outdoor opera performances, Italian food and wine, street performers, and a full Italian market. One of the city's most unique and beloved spring traditions.

title: Art Gallery & Live Performances
description: Downtown Knoxville's multi-discipline arts space at 100 S Gay St, featuring rotating gallery exhibitions, live performances, and artist studios. Free to visit the galleries. A hub for local and regional visual and performing artists.

title: Children's Science Museum
description: Knoxville's hands-on children's museum with rotating STEAM exhibits, maker spaces, a planetarium, and outdoor garden. Best for families with kids ages 2–12. Admission required; members free.

title: $19 Bottomless Cidermosas
description: $19 bottomless cidermosas every Saturday and Sunday 11 AM–4 PM at this Old North Knox cider and mead taproom. Hard cider in place of wine makes these a uniquely Knoxville twist on the classic brunch drink.
price: $19 per person · Sat & Sun 11 AM–4 PM

title: $20 Bottomless Mimosas Brunch
description: Bottomless mimosas ($20) every Saturday and Sunday during brunch at this South Knoxville neighborhood spot. Named for the street it sits on — one of Knoxville's dedicated brunch destinations.

title: $33 Sunday Seafood Brunch Buffet
description: Sunday brunch buffet 10:30 AM–2:30 PM: $33 adults, $15 children 12 & under. Fresh seafood bar, bloody mary bar, brunch entrees, and raw bar. One of Knoxville's most indulgent weekend experiences. Reservations recommended.

title: $1 Off All Drafts All Day Monday
description: $1 off ALL drafts all day Monday — plus daily happy hour Tue–Fri 3–6 PM. South Knoxville's best craft beer bar with an enormous tap list and a neighborhood vibe that can't be beat.
price: $1 off all drafts all day · Tue–Fri 3–6 PM too

title: $1 Nigiri & Yakitori Specials
description: Mon–Thu 3–5 PM: $1 nigiri and $1 yakitori small plates plus discounted sake, soju, and local brews. One of Knoxville's best-value happy hours — Japanese pub food at bar-snack prices in Old City.

title: Half Off Apps & $5 Wells Daily
description: Mon–Fri 4–6 PM: ½ off appetizers, $2 off beers & wines, $5 well spirits. Plus daily drink specials: Tini Tuesdays ($3 cocktails), Wine Wednesdays (½ off bottles), Tequila Thursdays ($6 margaritas).
price: Half off apps · $5 wells · Mon–Fri 4–6 PM

title: $3 Beers & $1.50 Tacos Daily
description: Mon–Fri 3–6:30 PM: $3 craft beers, $1.50 tacos & sliders, $3 chips & queso, $4 pimento & hummus plates. Knoxville's beloved craft brewery in a restored service station — happy hour food here is a steal.
price: $3 craft beers · $1.50 tacos & sliders · Mon–Fri 3–6:30 PM

title: $3 Drafts & Prosecco Happy Hour
description: Tue–Wed 5–7 PM, Thu–Fri 4–6 PM: $3 drafts, $3 prosecco, $5 house liquor. Knoxville's premier food hall with 11 restaurant concepts and a 40-seat bar — happy hour here means you can try multiple spots for the price of one.
price: $3 drafts & prosecco · Tue–Wed 5–7 PM · Thu–Fri 4–6 PM
